name: Roland, Paladin of Charlemagne
shortDescription: Roland was a legendary Frankish military leader under Charlemagne, celebrated for his valor and tragic death at the Battle of Roncevaux Pass. He is a central figure in the Matter of France, a cycle of medieval literature.
description: Roland, also known as Hruodland, was a historical figure who served as a military governor of the Breton March, a frontier region of the Frankish kingdom. However, he is best known through the legendary accounts that transformed him into a symbol of chivalry, loyalty, and Christian heroism. The most famous depiction of Roland is in the epic poem *The Song of Roland* (La Chanson de Roland), which recounts the Battle of Roncevaux Pass in 778 AD. In this narrative, Roland commands the rearguard of Charlemagne's army during its retreat from Spain. Betrayed by Ganelon, a treacherous knight, Roland and his men are ambushed by a vastly superior Saracen force. Despite the overwhelming odds, Roland refuses to call for help, choosing instead to fight to the death. He slays countless enemies with his sword, Durendal, and eventually blows his horn, Olifant, with such force that it bursts his temples, summoning Charlemagne but ultimately leading to his own demise. Roland's death becomes a powerful symbol of sacrifice, faith, and unwavering dedication to his king and his religion. His legend grew throughout the Middle Ages, inspiring countless works of art, literature, and music. He represents the ideal of the Christian knight, embodying courage, honor, and unwavering faith in the face of adversity.
quests: Defending the rearguard at Roncevaux Pass

sources.title: The Song of Roland (La Chanson de Roland)
sources.author: Unknown

sources.title: Medieval Literature: A Very Short Introduction
